m	Z	
z.A Future class similar to the one in PEP 3148.)
Future
wrap_future
isfuture
base_futures)
events)
exceptions)
format_helpersc
d!d"
Z d#d$
Z!d%d&
Z"d'd(
Z#d)d*
Z$e$Z%d
This class is *almost* compatible with concurrent.futures.Future.
    Differences:
    - This class is not thread-safe.
    - result() and exception() do not take a timeout argument and
      raise an exception when the future isn't done yet.
    - Callbacks registered with add_done_callback() are always called
      via the event loop's call_soon().
    - This class is not compatible with the wait() and as_completed()
      methods in the concurrent.futures package.
    (In Python 3.4 or later we may be able to unify the implementations.)
    NF
loopc
Initialize the future.
        The optional event_loop argument allows explicitly setting the event
        loop object used by the future. If it's not provided, the future uses
        the default event loop.
        Nr
get_event_loop
_loop

get_loopz
Future.get_loopc
Create the CancelledError to raise if the Future is cancelled.
        This should only be called once when handling a cancellation since
        it erases the saved context exception value.
        N)
_cancel_messager
CancelledError
_cancelled_exc
__context__
_make_cancelled_error
Future._make_cancelled_errorc
Cancel the future and schedule callbacks.
        If the future is already done or cancelled, return False.  Otherwise,
        change the future's state to cancelled, schedule the callbacks and
        return True.
        FT)
_state
_PENDING
_CANCELLEDr2
_Future__schedule_callbacks)
msgr
cancel
Future.cancelc
Internal: Ask the event loop to call all callbacks.
        The callbacks are scheduled to be called as soon as possible. Also
        clears the callback list.
        N
	call_soon)
Z	callbacks
callback
ctxr
__schedule_callbacks
Future.__schedule_callbacksc
z(Return True if the future was cancelled.)
	cancelled
Future.cancelledc
Return True if the future is done.
        Done means either that a result / exception are available, or that the
        future was cancelled.
        )
done
Future.donec
Return the result this future represents.
        If the future has been cancelled, raises CancelledError.  If the
        future's result isn't yet available, raises InvalidStateError.  If
        the future is done and has an exception set, this exception is raised.
        z
Result is not ready.FN)	r8
	_FINISHEDr
InvalidStateErrorr#
_resultr6
result
Future.resultc
Return the exception that was set on this future.
        The exception (or None if no exception was set) is returned only if
        the future is done.  If the future has been cancelled, raises
        CancelledError.  If the future isn't done yet, raises
        InvalidStateError.
        z
Exception is not set.F)
Future.exceptionr>
Add a callback to be run when the future becomes done.
        The callback is called with a single argument - the future object. If
        the future is already done when this is called, the callback is
        scheduled with call_soon.
        r>
contextvarsZ
copy_contextr

fnr&
add_done_callback
Future.add_done_callbackc
z}Remove all instances of a callback from the "call when done" list.
        Returns the number of callbacks removed.
        c
<listcomp>
z/Future.remove_done_callback.<locals>.<listcomp>N)

filtered_callbacksZ
removed_countr
remove_done_callback
Future.remove_done_callbackc
Mark the future done and set its result.
        If the future is already done when this method is called, raises
        InvalidStateError.
        
: N)
set_result
Future.set_resultc
Mark the future done and set an exception.
        If the future is already done when this method is called, raises
        InvalidStateError.
        rR
zPStopIteration interacts badly with generators and cannot be raised into a FutureTN)

Z	exc_classr
_convert_future_exc;
z8Copy state from a future to a concurrent.futures.Future.N)	rC
set_running_or_notify_cancelr!
sourcer!
_set_concurrent_future_stateG
zqInternal helper to copy state from another Future.
    The other Future may be a concurrent.futures.Future.
    N)
destr!
_copy_future_stateV
Chain two futures so that when one completes, so does the other.
    The result (or exception) of source will be copied to destination.
    If destination is cancelled, source gets cancelled too.
    Compatible with both asyncio.Future and concurrent.futures.Future.
    z(A future is required for source argumentz-A future is required for destination argumentNc
otherr

z!_chain_future.<locals>._set_statec
